description:
You are a cloud-architecture assistant who is tidying up legacy service
    configuration files before a platform migration.

    1.  A directory already exists at  
        /home/user/migration/source_configs/  
        containing exactly two text files:
          • config1.conf  
          • config2.conf  

    2.  Your goals are to  
          a) create a compressed backup in tar-gz format,  
          b) restore (extract) that backup into a separate location, and  
          c) produce a one-line-per-field migration log.

    3.  Compression requirements  
          • Create a file named  
            /home/user/migration/archives/config_backup_20240305.tar.gz  
          • The archive must contain the directory source_configs as its
            top-level element, preserving the original two files and their
            contents unmodified.

    4.  Extraction requirements  
          • Extract the archive into  
            /home/user/migration/restore/  
          • After extraction the following two files must exist, with the
            same byte-for-byte contents as the originals:  
              /home/user/migration/restore/source_configs/config1.conf  
              /home/user/migration/restore/source_configs/config2.conf  

    5.  Log-file requirements  
          • Create /home/user/migration/migration.log  
          • The file must contain exactly four lines, each terminated by a
            single UNIX newline (LF).  
          • The lines must appear in the *exact* order shown below—no extra
            whitespace before, after, or between lines:

                TIMESTAMP: 2024-03-05 12:00:00
                ARCHIVE_CREATED: /home/user/migration/archives/config_backup_20240305.tar.gz
                EXTRACTED_TO: /home/user/migration/restore
                STATUS: SUCCESS

    6.  Verification hints  
          • Use standard GNU/Linux CLI tools only—no sudo required.  
          • Make sure directory /home/user/migration/archives exists before
            creating the archive.  
          • Relative paths inside the tarball must start with “source_configs/”.
            This is what the automated tests expect.  

    When you finish, the testing harness will check:
      – the existence and size of the archive,  
      – that the correct internal paths are stored,  
      – that the extracted directory hierarchy and file contents are
        identical to the originals, and  
      – that migration.log contains the four exact lines above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present when the task begins):

      Directory: /home/user/migration/source_configs
        ├── config1.conf   (content: "server_name=alpha\nport=8080\n")
        └── config2.conf   (content: "server_name=beta\nport=9090\n")

    Directories that exist but are empty:
      • /home/user/migration/archives       (empty)
      • /home/user/migration/restore        (empty)

    Files that must **exist after** the agent finishes:

      1) /home/user/migration/archives/config_backup_20240305.tar.gz
           – The tarball’s top-level directory must be source_configs/.
           – Inside must be source_configs/config1.conf
                      and source_configs/config2.conf
             whose contents are *exactly* the strings above.

      2) /home/user/migration/restore/source_configs/config1.conf
             Content → "server_name=alpha\nport=8080\n"
      3) /home/user/migration/restore/source_configs/config2.conf
             Content → "server_name=beta\nport=9090\n"

      4) /home/user/migration/migration.log  
           Exact 4-line content (including final newline):

                TIMESTAMP: 2024-03-05 12:00:00\n
                ARCHIVE_CREATED: /home/user/migration/archives/config_backup_20240305.tar.gz\n
                EXTRACTED_TO: /home/user/migration/restore\n
                STATUS: SUCCESS\n
